questions.question: Which country has the most time zones?
questions.explanation: France has 12 time zones due to its overseas territories.

questions.question: What is the most spoken language in the world by native speakers?
questions.explanation: Mandarin Chinese has the most native speakers at over 900 million.

questions.question: Which element has the chemical symbol 'Au'?
questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin word 'aurum' meaning gold.

questions.question: How many keys does a standard piano have?
questions.explanation: A standard piano has 88 keys: 52 white and 36 black.

questions.question: Which country's flag has the most colors?
What year was the first iPhone released?
questions.explanation: The first iPhone was released on June 29, 2007.

questions.question: Which currency is used in Japan?
questions.explanation: The Japanese yen (¥) is the official currency of Japan.

questions.question: Who painted the Mona Lisa?
questions.explanation: Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa between 1503 and 1519.

questions.question: What is the most visited country in the world?
questions.explanation: France is the most visited country with over 89 million tourists per year.

questions.question: Which planet is named after the Roman god of war?
questions.explanation: Mars is named after the Roman god of war due to its red color.

questions.question: What is the largest country in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Russia is the largest country at 17.1 million km².

questions.question: Who invented the World Wide Web?
questions.explanation: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989 at CERN.

questions.question: Which is the only country to appear on every continent's border?
questions.explanation: No single country borders every continent.

questions.question: What is the hardest natural substance?
questions.explanation: Diamond is the hardest natural substance, rated 10 on the Mohs scale.

questions.question: In which year did World War II end?
questions.explanation: WWII ended in 1945 with the surrender of Germany and Japan.
